name: zeptoforth
summary: Modern Cortex-M Forth mixing subroutine threading, native code inlining, and a preemptively multitasking RTOS. Leans toward 'serious embedded application environment' while remaining performance-conscious — most tiny Forths stay minimal.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: **zeptoforth** is a modern Cortex-M Forth that mixes subroutine threading, native code inlining, and a preemptively multitasking RTOS. It is interesting because most tiny Forths stay minimal, but zeptoforth leans toward "serious embedded application environment" while remaining performance-conscious.

sections.title: Position
sections.content: zeptoforth's combination of native codegen, RTOS multitasking, and a Forth interactive console makes it more than a minimal Forth — it is positioned as a development environment for non-trivial Cortex-M applications. The trade-off is footprint: zeptoforth is larger than Mecrisp's classic minimal implementations, but offers a richer programming environment in exchange.
